name: Routing Log (Postgres)
description: Per-turn routing decisions: which model processed each field, the sensitivity tag applied, the reason, and the outcome. The source of truth for the Model Routing Provenance Trace — proves sensitive data stayed in the SLM.

name: LiteLLM Gateway
description: Unified model gateway in front of the in-environment SLM and Claude Sonnet LLM. Emits token counts, model split, semantic cache hit rate, and per-turn cost — the feed behind tokenomics and cost governance.

name: KAG Knowledge Graph (Neo4j)
description: The governance graph: field nodes, source-system nodes, and policy nodes (rules like DG-07) with their relationships. Decides what is sensitive and where it originates; queried via Cypher to drive the routing decision. Re-rendered in Ultra through Gen UI — never shown as the raw Neo4j browser.

name: Agent Observability Log
description: Per-turn record of every agent action: query, response, context sources used, chain-of-thought, and the policy or business rule that drove it. Powers the single-pane observability panel — rendered conversationally, not as raw Grafana.

name: LiteLLM Cost Records (Postgres)
description: Per-query and per-person token cost records with SLM/LLM split and savings versus LLM-only processing. Governs cost over time across every persona on the platform.

name: Model Registry
description: Registry of models in production: the in-environment SLM (Azure) and Claude Sonnet LLM (Azure), with versions, deployment environment, and routing eligibility.

name: Grafana (via API / MCP)
description: Infrastructure telemetry for the admin portal — router throughput, CPU, and p95 latency. Pulled through an API/MCP call and re-rendered as a Gen UI chart in Ultra rather than embedding raw Grafana.
